name: Lisbon: From Commerce Square to the Castle

stops.name: Praça do Comércio
stops.shortDescription: A magnificent, sun-drenched square facing the Tagus River. It was built on the ruins of the old Royal Palace, which was destroyed by the catastrophic 1755 Lisbon earthquake.

stops.name: Rua Augusta Arch
stops.shortDescription: A stone, triumphal arch-like building commemorating the city's reconstruction after the 1755 earthquake. It serves as an ornate gateway into Baixa, the vibrant downtown grid.

stops.name: Santa Justa Lift
stops.shortDescription: A beautifully intricate, 19th-century Neo-Gothic iron elevator created by an apprentice of Gustave Eiffel. It vertically connects the lower streets of the Baixa with the higher Carmo Square.

stops.name: Lisbon Cathedral (Sé)
stops.shortDescription: The oldest church in the city, built in 1147 right after the Christian reconquest of Lisbon. Its fortress-like, Romanesque facade is iconic, complete with rose windows and heavy twin bell towers.

stops.name: Miradouro de Santa Luzia
stops.shortDescription: A romantic observation deck offering sweeping views over the terracotta roofs of Alfama and the Tagus River. The terrace is beautifully decorated with traditional blue and white azulejo tiles.

stops.name: Castelo de São Jorge
stops.shortDescription: A Moorish castle towering majestically over Lisbon's historic center. Walking its ancient, pine-shaded ramparts rewards you with arguably the most breathtaking panoramic views of the entire city.
